a: The estimate transforms employment shares into wage-weighted task exposure.

rho: Implementation varies with scan-tool access, manufacturer information, and specialty workflow standardization.
rho: Written estimates and customer authorization require controlled review.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repair orders paid by consumers, fleets, warranty administrators, or insurers, with revenue from specialist labor, diagnostics, parts markup, and related shop charges.
lens.excluded_activities: General automotive repair, engine repair and replacement, dealership service departments, oil-change-only shops, body and glass shops, captive fleet garages, parts retailers, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market firms providing repeat specialized mechanical or electrical repair services, including brake, suspension, exhaust, transmission, electrical, and tune-up work, to external vehicle owners and fleet customers.

a.bridge: BLS measures employment and wages for the broad automotive repair and maintenance group rather than specialized repair tasks. I map parts of management, sales, service writing, customer service, bookkeeping, office work, fault-code interpretation, technical knowledge retrieval, and estimate documentation to AI exposure while excluding physical inspection and repair. Specialized shops may have a higher technician share than the broad group, biasing exposure downward after adjustment if diagnostic reasoning is highly augmentable and upward if nearly all diagnostic work must remain on-vehicle.
caveats: The occupation source covers NAICS 8111 and excludes self-employed workers.
Brake, transmission, exhaust, suspension, and electrical shops can have materially different task mixes.
The estimate transforms employment shares into wage-weighted task exposure.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task diary by specialty, covering diagnostic reasoning, hands-on work, service writing, parts, administration, and customer contact, would materially change the estimate.
a.rationale: Specialized repair concentrates skilled physical work, but AI can assist diagnostic triage, technical-information retrieval, estimate preparation, parts matching, scheduling, warranty documentation, and customer explanation.

d5.bridge: BLS projects modest employment growth for automotive mechanical and electrical repair while FHWA projects continued light-duty mileage growth; Energy Department guidance shows all-electric vehicles need less maintenance and have reduced brake wear, fewer fluids, and fewer moving parts. I translate those broad drivers to the current specialized-repair basket, with sharper downside for exhaust, transmission, and tune-up specialties but continued demand for suspension, brakes, electrical systems, tires, and aging combustion vehicles. Broad repair projections may overstate legacy-specialty demand, while fleet turnover and new electrical complexity may make them understate adaptable shops.
caveats: BLS projections are published above the six-digit specialty code.
FHWA mileage is a demand driver rather than direct repair quantity.
The range blends specialties with structurally different exposure to electric vehicles.
d5.change_evidence: Constant-price repair-order counts and labor hours by specialty, powertrain, and vehicle age, combined with a local in-service fleet transition forecast, would materially change the ratio.
d5.rationale: The installed combustion fleet and growing mileage support near-term repair demand, but drivetrain electrification changes or eliminates several services explicitly included in this code. Specialty mix and ability to retrain determine whether current service quantity holds.

caveats: The frozen firm count is missing and must not be inferred from Census firm or establishment totals.
Census reported 7,708 firms and 8,042 establishments in 2022 across all sizes, not the screened band.
Eligibility may differ materially across specialties.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level roster with specialty, external-customer revenue, repeat repair-order history, affiliation, parts-retail share, and normalized EBITDA would establish eligibility.
e.rationale: The classification is predominantly external repair service, so most firms in the specified EBITDA band should fit the recurring or repeat-service lens. Mixed parts retail, captive or dealer-affiliated operations, and project-style rebuilders can fall outside it.

l.detail: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) at ancestor 8111: labor compensation (L02) $79,569.3M / sectoral output (T30) $196,358.2M, year 2024

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[81]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $185,876 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 81111); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: Demand eliminated by drivetrain change is assigned to d5 and should not be counted again here.
An operator may remain necessary while the work migrates to dealer or manufacturer-controlled networks.
Operator necessity differs substantially across the code's specialties.
o.change_evidence: Job-code analysis of work eliminated by over-the-air updates, manufacturer-direct service, owner self-service, modular replacement, and mobile diagnostics, separated by specialty, would materially change this share.
o.rationale: Most surviving year-five specialty repair quantity requires an accountable operator with lifts, tools, parts, testing equipment, and safety responsibility. Remote diagnostics and software updates can eliminate or redirect some electrical and tune-up work, but physical brake, suspension, exhaust, and transmission work cannot be supplied by software alone.

caveats: FTC guidance describes consumer billing but not benefit pass-through.
Pricing power differs between scarce electrical or transmission expertise and more comparable brake or exhaust work.
Warranty and fleet contracts can retain more of the savings for the payer.
q.change_evidence: Repair-order cohorts by specialty linking realized process savings to quoted hours, labor rates, diagnostic fees, parts margins, contract renewals, discounting, and customer retention would materially change retention.
q.rationale: Specialist labor rates, diagnostics, flat-rate times, and parts markups permit initial retention where expertise is scarce and switching during a repair is costly. Transparent estimates, general-repair substitutes, fleet and warranty terms, and local competition erode part of the benefit over five years.

caveats: No source directly measures five-year AI implementation for specialized repair firms.
Implementation varies with scan-tool access, manufacturer information, and specialty workflow standardization.
Written estimates and customer authorization require controlled review.
rho.change_evidence: Production pilots by specialty and shop-management stack reporting adoption, override rates, diagnostic accuracy, estimate cycle time, labor hours, comebacks, and safety incidents would materially change this range.
rho.rationale: Administrative workflows can adopt quickly, while diagnostic assistance depends on vehicle data access, tool integration, specialty-specific accuracy, technician acceptance, and traceable human review. Safety-critical brake, steering, suspension, electrical, and transmission decisions limit unattended implementation.

s5.bridge: SBA summarizes Census evidence across all employer businesses that 22 percent of owners acquired a purchased business and 64.5 percent planned to sell. I use these broad indicators to bound completed qualifying control transfers within five years for eligible specialized repair firms, discounting unbounded sale intentions, closures, gifts, inheritances, and internal changes. The raw broad-owner figures likely overstate realized five-year transfers; specialty scarcity and strategic buyer interest could make the adjusted estimate conservative for established shops.
caveats: The source is all-industry and does not measure five-year realized control transfers.
The missing frozen firm count prevents translating this conditional probability into a target count.
Internal succession and asset-only liquidation are not automatically qualifying transfers.
s5.change_evidence: A five-year matched panel of eligible specialty shops using ownership filings, lender records, business-broker closings, transaction databases, and direct owner interviews would materially change the probability.
s5.rationale: A specialized shop can transfer valuable technicians, equipment, certifications, referral relationships, and local reputation, but no specialty-specific transfer denominator was found and seller intention is not a completed transaction.

principal_driver: Specialist technical knowledge and recurring physical repair needs support operator relevance and selective AI augmentation.
principal_weakness: The code mixes specialties with sharply different exposure to electric-drivetrain substitution, and the frozen firm count is missing.
